sections.content: 2 cells changed this time. Let me check what changed - was it just the pink bar shrinking again, or did something else happen?

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking on the charcoal diamond area
if previous_frame:
    prev_ascii = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    curr_ascii = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    
    diffs = []
    for r in range(64):
        for c in range(64):
            if r < len(prev_ascii) and r < len(curr_ascii) and c < len(prev_ascii[r]) and c < len(curr_ascii[r]):
                if prev_ascii[r][c] != curr_ascii[r][c]:
                    diffs.append((r, c, prev_ascii[r][c], curr_ascii[r][c]))
    
    print(f"Total cell diffs: {len(diffs)}")
    for r, c, old, new in diffs:
        print(f"  ({r},{c}): {old} -> {new}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Actually, let me reconsider. Both clicks so far were on "background" cells (gray G and charcoal c). Maybe I need to click on the "foreground" objects like the black bars, yellow strips, or blue blocks. Let me try clicking on the black horizontal bar.
